title: container started with podman compose can't resolve dns names of others container in the same network
body:
### Issue Description

Hello, this is a regression. I have a simple docker compose file with pgadmin and a postgres instance. When configuring pgadmin I usually put the name of the container that runs the database, this usually works and resolves it to the correct ip addres but it doesn't work anymore with the latest update.
The latest versions where I know that everything was working fine are:
podman 5.4.2-2
podman-compose 1.3.0-1
netavark 1.14.1-1
aardvark-dns 1.14.0-1

With these versions it doesn't work anymore:
podman 5.5.0-1
podman-compose 1.4.0-1
netavark 1.15.0-1
aardvark-dns 1.15.0-1

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Here it is my docker file:
```docker
services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:alpine3.18
    restart: always
    ports:
      - 5432:5432
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: agendacloud
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
      POSTGRES_USER: postgres
    volumes:
      - postgres:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    networks:
        - db_net

  pgadmin:
    image: dpage/pgadmin4:latest
    environment:
      PGADMIN_DEFAULT_EMAIL: admin@pgadmin.com
      PGADMIN_DEFAULT_PASSWORD: admin
      PGADMIN_LISTEN_PORT: 80
      PGADMIN_CONFIG_MASTER_PASSWORD_REQUIRED: 'False'
      PGADMIN_CONFIG_SERVER_MODE: 'False'
    ports:
      - 15432:80
    volumes:
      - pgadmin:/var/lib/pgadmin:U
    depends_on:
      - postgres
    networks:
        - db_net

volumes:
  postgres:
  pgadmin:

networks:
  db_net:
```
Just download and run `podman compose up`, then open the browser on `http://localhost:15432/` and click on add server. Put a name and then in the connection tab put postgres as hostname and postgres as password.
Try to connect and you can see an error that says connection refused from localhost.

### Describe the results you received

It can't resolve the database container hostname. Instead it says connection refused from localhost.
```
connection failed: connection to server at "127.0.0.1", port 5432 failed: Connection refused
	Is the server running on that host and accepting TCP/IP connections?
`

### Describe the results you expected

I expect a connection.

### podman info output

```yaml

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional information

comments.body:
UPDATE:
Upon further testing and thinkering with some version I think I've found the culprit. Everything works fine with these versions:
```
podman 5.5.0-1
podman-compose 1.4.0-1
netavark 1.14.1-1
aardvark-dns 1.15.0-1
```
But when I update netavark  to `netavark-1.15.0-1` the bug it's present so is definitely something with that update. I don't know if I should open a issue in the netavark github because maybe this is a problem with podman or podman compose. I would like to add that if I use a pod everything works as expected.
It isn't a networking problem because if I get the correct ip from the network inspect command the connection works.

can you share the resulting /etc/resolv.conf in the container?

Only related dns changes in netavark were https://github.com/containers/netavark/pull/1236 and https://github.com/containers/netavark/pull/1214 where the later might be the cause for you. Although I tested dns lookups and the search domain didn't seem needed

Does it work if you set the search domain to `dns.podman` for the container?
It is not clear what pgadmin does for dns lookups, if I try with `ping` and `getent ahosts` they lookup the name just fine.

Can you creatable a simpler reproducer? compose is not support by us and if I try this it seems to work correctly:

```
$ podman network create n1
n1
$ podman run --rm --network n1 --name c1 -d quay.io/libpod/testimage:20241011 sleep 1000
4cf9a22f72d8c6eda0ca82b33e308dbf2bbff35bd883ccdbde5c4cb75c64cd83
$ podman run --rm --network n1 --name test quay.io/libpod/testimage:20241011 ping -c 1 c1
PING c1 (10.89.0.2): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 10.89.0.2: seq=0 ttl=42 time=0.037 ms

--- c1 ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 0.037/0.037/0.037 ms
```

Ok the problems is with this line:
`search homenet.telecomitalia.it`
This was added by the DHCP server in my ISP router. Telling NetworkManager to ignore the search domain provided in the DHCP config (by selecting DHCP addresses only) removes that line from `/etc/resolv.conf` and now the hostname resolution works as expected.
So I think that is the expected behavior and everything was working fine. Since my ISP has an entry on their search domain it was returning that ip address. Maybe it should prioritize the local nameserver instead of the search domain in the containers?

Does it work if you addd `options ndots:0`? I think that should make it lookup the name without domain part first which aardvark-dns should respond too. But yeah it makes sense if the search domain returns a hit then it uses it and does not try without search domain

I think docker forces ndots:0 so likely have to do the same cc @mheon
Yes, by setting `options ndots:0` it works with the search domain.

++bump I have to manually go in and set the ndots option to get networking working again glad it’s not just me

Would really like this to be automatically applied

Yes of course that is the plan. We try to fix a regression quickly so I try to fix it tomorrow and then I guess together with some other critical bugs/regressions we make a podman 5.4.1 either later this week or next week maybe.

Thank you! Y’all are heroes for finding this I was tearing my hair out trying to figure this out

(I’m running tailscale with magic dns which works via dns search domain entry) - adding that detail here to help anyone else track this issue down when searching

This will hit if ur using tailscale magic dns not just an isp with dhcp entries

I noticed a similar (same?) issue with netavark-1.15.0-1: When I start the containers before connecting to a VPN the containers correctly resolve the IP adresses of the containers (and continues to work after connecting to the VPN). 

But if I first connect to a VPN (that changes the /etc/resolv.conf on the host to include a new name server) and then start the containers the containers resolve the wrong IPs. In this case the VPN name server does resolve the name db and so the traffic is sent to an external IP and not to the container. This example does not work with the VPN. Adding .dns.podman fixes the issue. 

```podman network create n1
podman run --rm --network n1 --name db -d quay.io/libpod/testimage:20241011 sleep 1000
podman run --rm --network n1 --name test quay.io/libpod/testimage:20241011 ping -c 1 db
```

Just as update, I decided to just revert the change in netavark and will release 1.15.1 there soon. Then I have to fix the problem I originally tried to fix with the search domain removal in a different way which is fine.

At least for the fedora users please test the updates and give karma so this can land in stable sooner
F42 https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2025-d9889cbb72
F41 https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2025-77d31c7fe8
